questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Assertion (A): When a wire is stretched to double its length, its resistance becomes four times.
Reason (R): Resistance is directly proportional to length and inversely proportional to cross-sectional area.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

practice-questions.questionText: What is the time complexity of binary search?
practice-questions.solution: Binary search repeatedly halves the search space, so it takes at most log₂(n) comparisons, giving O(log n) time complexity.
practice-questions.subject: Computer Science

practice-questions.questionText: Which of the following are sorting algorithms?
practice-questions.correctAnswer: Merge Sort, Quick Sort, Heap Sort
practice-questions.solution: Merge Sort, Quick Sort, and Heap Sort are all comparison-based sorting algorithms. Binary Search is a searching algorithm, not a sorting one.
practice-questions.subject: Computer Science

practice-questions.questionText: What is the value of 2^10?
practice-questions.subject: Mathematics

practice-questions.questionText: Which data structures use LIFO (Last In First Out) ordering?
practice-questions.correctAnswer: Stack, Call Stack, Deque (used as stack)
practice-questions.solution: Stack and Call Stack strictly follow LIFO. A Deque can be used as a stack (LIFO). A Queue follows FIFO (First In First Out).
practice-questions.subject: Computer Science

practice-questions.questionText: What is the derivative of sin(x)?
practice-questions.solution: By standard differentiation rules, d/dx [sin(x)] = cos(x).
practice-questions.subject: Mathematics

practice-questions.questionText: Newton's second law states F = ma. If mass = 5 kg and acceleration = 3 m/s², what is the force in Newtons?
practice-questions.subject: Physics

practice-questions.questionText: Which of the following are properties of a Binary Search Tree (BST)?
options: Left child is smaller than parent
Right child is greater than parent
All nodes have exactly two children
practice-questions.correctAnswer: Left child is smaller than parent, Right child is greater than parent, In-order traversal gives sorted output
practice-questions.solution: A BST requires left < parent < right at each node, and in-order traversal visits nodes in ascending order. Nodes do NOT need to have exactly two children.
practice-questions.subject: Computer Science

practice-questions.questionText: Which of the following is the correct recurrence relation for Merge Sort?
practice-questions.solution: Merge Sort divides the array into 2 halves (2T(n/2)) and merges them in O(n) time, giving T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n), which resolves to O(n log n).
practice-questions.subject: Computer Science

practice-questions.questionText: What is the integral of 1/x dx?
practice-questions.solution: The integral of 1/x is the natural logarithm: ∫(1/x)dx = ln|x| + C
practice-questions.subject: Mathematics

practice-questions.questionText: A resistor of 10Ω is connected to a 5V battery. What is the current in Amperes?
practice-questions.solution: Using Ohm's Law: I = V/R = 5/10 = 0.5 A
practice-questions.subject: Physics

practice-questions.questionText: Which of the following are valid HTTP methods?
practice-questions.solution: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS are valid HTTP methods. SEND is not a standard HTTP method.
practice-questions.subject: Computer Science

practice-questions.questionText: What is the electric field inside a hollow charged spherical conductor?
practice-questions.solution: By Gauss's Law, the electric field inside a closed conducting shell is zero, as there is no enclosed charge inside the shell.
practice-questions.subject: Physics

practice-questions.questionText: How many prime numbers are there between 1 and 20?
practice-questions.solution: The prime numbers between 1 and 20 are: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19 — a total of 8 primes.
practice-questions.subject: Mathematics

practice-questions.questionText: Which of the following are object-oriented programming principles?
practice-questions.solution: The four pillars of OOP are Encapsulation, Abstraction, Polymorphism, and Inheritance. Recursion is a programming technique, not an OOP principle.
practice-questions.subject: Computer Science

practice-questions.questionText: In a triangle, if two angles are 60° and 70°, what is the third angle in degrees?
practice-questions.solution: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. Third angle = 180 - 60 - 70 = 50°
practice-questions.subject: Mathematics

practice-questions.questionText: Which traversal of a BST gives nodes in sorted (ascending) order?
practice-questions.solution: In-order traversal visits Left → Root → Right. For a BST, this always yields nodes in ascending sorted order.
practice-questions.subject: Computer Science

practice-questions.questionText: Which of the following are thermodynamic processes?
practice-questions.solution: Isothermal (constant temperature), Adiabatic (no heat exchange), and Isochoric (constant volume) are all thermodynamic processes. Electromagnetic is not a thermodynamic process.
practice-questions.subject: Physics

practice-questions.questionText: What is the space complexity of Merge Sort?
practice-questions.solution: Merge Sort requires O(n) auxiliary space for the temporary arrays used during the merge step.
practice-questions.subject: Computer Science

practice-questions.questionText: What is the limit of (sin x)/x as x approaches 0?
practice-questions.solution: This is a standard limit: lim(x→0) sin(x)/x = 1. It can be proven using L'Hôpital's Rule or the squeeze theorem.
practice-questions.subject: Mathematics

practice-questions.questionText: A projectile is launched horizontally at 20 m/s. After 3 seconds, what is the horizontal distance covered in meters? (Ignore air resistance)
practice-questions.solution: Horizontal motion is uniform: distance = speed × time = 20 × 3 = 60 m
practice-questions.subject: Physics
